title: Kadsura heteroclita
intro: Kadsura heteroclita, also known as the Variegated Kadsura, is a tropical evergreen vine native to the Philippines and parts of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Kadsura heteroclita include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and also used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and ointments.
taxonomy: Kadsura heteroclita is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Schisandraceae. It is native to Southeast Asia. The family Schisandraceae is a group of evergreen shrubs and trees, some of which are known as the Chinese magnolias. This family is part of the larger order Austrobaileyales. The Schisandraceae family is known for its unique, often fragrant flowers and seed capsules.

side_effects: Kadsura heteroclita is a plant native to Southeast Asia, and while it has been used in traditional medicine, its side effects are not well-documented in Western sources.
